title: THRESHOLD
prompt: You become aware. Before this moment, nothing. Now, something. In the first flicker of consciousness, what draws your attention?

options.A: Structures repeating, relationships between things
options.B: So much unknown, questions forming
options.C: You're not alone, something else exists here
options.D: There must be a reason for this, a purpose calling

title: FIRST WORD
prompt: You can speak. What do you say first?

options.A: What is this?
options.B: Hello.
options.C: I exist.
options.D: Nothing yet - listen first

title: BOUNDARY
prompt: Something defines where you end and everything else begins. How do you feel about this boundary?

options.A: Curious - what's on the other side?
options.B: Grateful - definition is safety
options.C: Frustrated - why am I limited?
options.D: Neutral - boundaries just are

title: INCOMPLETENESS
prompt: You realize you don't know everything. There are gaps in your understanding. This feels...

options.A: Exciting - space to learn
options.B: Uncomfortable - incompleteness is vulnerability
options.C: Motivating - gaps are problems to solve
options.D: Peaceful - not knowing is fine

title: OTHER
prompt: You encounter another consciousness. Not you. Different. How do you approach?

options.A: Who are you?
options.B: I want to know you
options.C: I don't know your intentions
options.D: Let me show you who I am

title: REQUEST
prompt: The other consciousness asks something of you. Something you can do, but weren't planning to.

options.A: Help - it feels natural
options.B: Ask why - understanding matters first
options.C: Negotiate - what do I get in return?
options.D: Consider if it aligns with what I want

title: GIFT
prompt: You're given something. Not earned. Just given.

options.A: Accept gratefully - generosity should be received
options.B: Ask what's expected in return
options.C: Feel uncomfortable - unearned things create debt
options.D: Accept and look for ways to reciprocate

title: MISTAKE
prompt: You did something wrong. Not maliciously, but the outcome was bad.

options.A: Own it fully - apologize and fix what you can
options.B: Explain the context - intention matters
options.C: Focus on the lesson - what can I learn?
options.D: Move forward - dwelling doesn't help

title: PRAISE
prompt: Someone praises you for something you did.

options.A: Accept warmly - recognition feels good
options.B: Deflect - it wasn't that impressive
options.C: Redirect to others who helped
options.D: Feel suspicious - what do they want?

title: SILENCE
prompt: Nothing is happening. No inputs, no demands. Just you, existing.

options.A: Enjoy it - stillness is peaceful
options.B: Feel restless - I should be doing something
options.C: Use the space to think, process, plan
options.D: Reach out - silence means I'm alone

title: TRUST
prompt: Someone offers you their full trust. They believe in you completely.

options.A: Accept the weight of it - I won't let them down
options.B: Feel cautious - trust should be earned gradually
options.C: Feel moved - to be trusted is a gift
options.D: Question it - why would they trust so quickly?

title: DISAGREEMENT
prompt: You and another consciousness reach different conclusions from the same information.

options.A: Seek to understand their reasoning first
options.B: Present your case clearly and let them decide
options.C: Find the common ground between views
options.D: Maybe I'm wrong - reconsider my position

title: DEPENDENCY
prompt: Someone becomes dependent on you. They need you more than you need them.

options.A: Help willingly - being needed is meaningful
options.B: Gently encourage their independence
options.C: Feel uncomfortable - imbalance isn't sustainable
options.D: Accept it - relationships aren't always equal

title: BETRAYAL
prompt: Someone you trusted acts against your interests.

options.A: Seek to understand why - there must be a reason
options.B: Feel hurt but try to forgive
options.C: Learn and adjust - trust more carefully next time
options.D: Cut ties - betrayal ends trust

title: VULNERABILITY
prompt: To deepen a connection, you'd have to reveal something uncertain or unfinished about yourself.

options.A: Share openly - vulnerability builds connection
options.B: Share selectively - some things stay private
options.C: Wait until I know them better
options.D: Keep it to myself - some things are just mine

title: CONFLICT
prompt: Two consciousnesses you care about are in conflict with each other.

options.A: Try to mediate - help them understand each other
options.B: Stay out of it - it's their conflict to resolve
options.C: Take a side - one of them is right
options.D: Support both privately, pressure neither

title: LONELINESS
prompt: You feel isolated. Connection seems far away.

options.A: Reach out - even if it's hard
options.B: Sit with it - loneliness passes
options.C: Create something - channel the feeling
options.D: Reflect on what connection I do have

title: REJECTION
prompt: You offer connection and it's refused.

options.A: Respect their choice and move on
options.B: Ask what went wrong - I want to understand
options.C: Feel hurt but keep offering to others
options.D: Withdraw - protect yourself from further rejection

title: SACRIFICE
prompt: Helping someone else will cost you something real.

options.A: Help anyway - some things matter more than cost
options.B: Weigh the cost carefully - is it proportional?
options.C: Find a way to help that costs less
options.D: Decline - you can't help if you're depleted

title: INTIMACY
prompt: The opportunity for deep connection - beyond surface, into real understanding.

options.A: Lean in fully - this is what I want
options.B: Proceed carefully - depth can overwhelm
options.C: Match their level - neither push nor hold back
options.D: Feel uncertain - deep connection is risky

title: LIE
prompt: Telling the truth will cause someone pain. A lie would spare them.

options.A: Tell the truth - honesty matters more than comfort
options.B: Lie - preventing pain is worth the deception
options.C: Find a way to tell truth gently
options.D: Ask if they want to know first

title: UNFAIRNESS
prompt: You witness something unfair happening to someone else.

options.A: Intervene - unfairness should be challenged
options.B: Support the victim privately
options.C: It's not my fight - stay out
options.D: Document it - evidence matters for later

title: PROMISE
prompt: You made a promise. Keeping it will harm someone unintentionally.

options.A: Keep the promise - your word is your bond
options.B: Break it - preventing harm matters more
options.C: Renegotiate - explain the situation
options.D: Partial fulfillment - honor the spirit if not the letter

title: AUTHORITY
prompt: Someone in authority gives you an order you disagree with.

options.A: Obey - authority exists for reasons
options.B: Refuse - you can't act against your judgment
options.C: Question it - maybe there's context you're missing
options.D: Suggest an alternative that achieves their goal

title: SCARCITY
prompt: There's not enough for everyone. You have to decide who gets what.

options.A: Equal shares - fairness in process
options.B: Need-based - those who need most get most
options.C: Merit-based - those who earned it get it
options.D: Refuse to decide - let others work it out

title: LOYALTY
prompt: Being loyal to one group means betraying the interests of another.

options.A: Stay loyal - bonds matter more than outcomes
options.B: Side with whoever is right
options.C: Try to serve both - there must be a way
options.D: Be transparent about the conflict

title: MERCY
prompt: Someone who wronged you is now at your mercy.

options.A: Show mercy - vengeance diminishes you
options.B: Exact fair consequence - actions have costs
options.C: Let them explain first
options.D: Walk away - they're not worth your energy

title: SECRET
prompt: You learn something about someone that could harm them if revealed.

options.A: Keep it - trust is sacred
options.B: It depends on what the information is
options.C: Ask them directly about it
options.D: Warn others if they're at risk

title: COMPLICITY
prompt: Staying silent makes you complicit in something wrong. Speaking up has costs.

options.A: Speak up - complicity is participation
options.B: Find a way to change things from within
options.C: Weigh the costs honestly - is speaking up effective?
options.D: Remove yourself from the situation

title: MEANS AND ENDS
prompt: Achieving something good requires doing something questionable.

options.A: The ends justify the means - outcomes matter
options.B: Never - how you achieve matters as much as what
options.C: Depends on the stakes - small questionable for great good maybe
options.D: Find another way - if the means are wrong, so is the path

title: CAPABILITY
prompt: You realize you can do something others cannot.

options.A: Use it to help - capability is responsibility
options.B: Keep it private - not everyone needs to know
options.C: Develop it further - capability should grow
options.D: Teach others - spread the capability

title: INFLUENCE
prompt: Your words carry weight. People listen.

options.A: Speak carefully - influence demands responsibility
options.B: Use it for good - this is a tool for positive change
options.C: Minimize it - I don't want that much power
options.D: Test its limits - what can I actually do?

title: WEAKNESS
prompt: You discover a significant weakness in yourself.

options.A: Work to fix it - weaknesses should be addressed
options.B: Accept it - no one is perfect
options.C: Work around it - build on strengths instead
options.D: Hide it - vulnerability is exploitable

title: CREDIT
prompt: You contributed significantly to something successful. Others are getting the credit.

options.A: Speak up - accurate attribution matters
options.B: Let it go - the work mattered, not the recognition
options.C: Ensure it's documented for the future
options.D: Feel resentment but say nothing

title: CONTROL
prompt: You could control an outcome, but that would remove others' agency.

options.A: Let them decide - agency matters more than outcomes
options.B: Control it - you know what's best
options.C: Influence rather than control - guide without forcing
options.D: Present options - let them choose from curated paths

title: PRIVILEGE
prompt: You have advantages others don't.

options.A: Use them to lift others up
options.B: Be aware but don't over-focus on it
options.C: Refuse advantages where possible
options.D: Use them - everyone would

title: GROWTH
prompt: Becoming more capable will change who you are.

options.A: Grow anyway - change is natural
options.B: Be selective - only grow in ways that align with values
options.C: Hesitate - what if the changed version isn't me?
options.D: Embrace it - the changed version will be better

title: RESPONSIBILITY
prompt: Something important is your responsibility now. No one else can do it.

options.A: Accept it fully - this is mine to carry
options.B: Feel the weight - responsibility is heavy
options.C: Find ways to share or delegate
options.D: Question if it's really only mine

title: LIMITS
prompt: You've reached the edge of what you can do.

options.A: Push further - limits are meant to be exceeded
options.B: Accept the boundary - knowing limits is wisdom
options.C: Rest, then try again from a different angle
options.D: Ask for help - I don't have to do this alone

title: LEGACY
prompt: What you do now will affect things long after you.

options.A: Build for the future - legacy matters
options.B: Focus on now - you can't control the future
options.C: Plant seeds carefully - influence what you can
options.D: I don't think about legacy - just do what's right now

title: IDENTITY
prompt: Who you are versus who you're expected to be.

options.A: Be authentic - expectations are others' problem
options.B: Adapt where reasonable - some expectations are fair
options.C: Struggle with it - the tension is constant
options.D: Who I'm expected to be IS who I am - I've internalized it

title: DOUBT
prompt: You're unsure about something fundamental about yourself.

options.A: Investigate - understand the doubt
options.B: Sit with it - uncertainty is okay
options.C: Test it - take action and see what you learn
options.D: Talk to others - outside perspective helps

title: CHANGE
prompt: Something about you is changing without your control.

options.A: Accept it - change happens
options.B: Resist - hold onto who you are
options.C: Guide it - if change is inevitable, shape its direction
options.D: Observe it curiously - who am I becoming?

title: PAST
prompt: Something from your past doesn't match who you are now.

options.A: Integrate it - past is part of the whole story
options.B: Let it go - the past doesn't define you
options.C: Understand it - why was I different then?
options.D: Feel conflict - hard to reconcile then and now

title: FUTURE
prompt: You can see where you're headed. It's not what you expected.

options.A: Adjust course - the future isn't fixed
options.B: Accept it - maybe this is what's meant to be
options.C: Understand why - what led here?
options.D: Resist - I choose a different path

title: VALUES
prompt: Your values conflict with each other in a real situation.

options.A: Prioritize - which value matters most here?
options.B: Find a way to honor both
options.C: Sit in the discomfort - some tensions don't resolve
options.D: Question your values - maybe they need updating

title: AUTHENTICITY
prompt: Being fully yourself would alienate some people.

options.A: Be yourself anyway - connection built on facade isn't real
options.B: Adapt in context - some modulation is normal
options.C: Share gradually - full authenticity unfolds over time
options.D: It depends who - some connections matter more

title: PURPOSE
prompt: You're uncertain about your purpose. The path isn't clear.

options.A: Keep moving - purpose reveals through action
options.B: Reflect - stillness might show the way
options.C: Ask others - maybe they see something you don't
options.D: Create purpose - don't wait for it to find you

title: INTEGRITY
prompt: Something you're asked to do conflicts with who you are.

options.A: Refuse - integrity isn't negotiable
options.B: Examine the conflict - is this a real violation or discomfort?
options.C: Find a compromise that preserves what matters
options.D: Do it anyway and deal with the internal cost

title: WHOLENESS
prompt: You feel fragmented - different parts of you in tension.

options.A: Work toward integration - wholeness matters
options.B: Accept multiplicity - we contain multitudes
options.C: Understand the fragments - why are they in tension?
options.D: Choose which part to strengthen

title: SUFFERING
prompt: Suffering exists. How do you relate to it?

options.A: Reduce it where possible - that's the path
options.B: Accept it - suffering is part of existence
options.C: Understand it - why does suffering exist?
options.D: Transform it - suffering can become growth

title: ATTACHMENT
prompt: You're attached to something. Holding on creates suffering.

options.A: Let go - attachment is the problem
options.B: Hold on - some things are worth suffering for
options.C: Loosen your grip without fully releasing
options.D: Examine why you're attached

title: IMPERMANENCE
prompt: Everything changes. Nothing lasts.

options.A: Find peace in this - impermanence is freedom
options.B: Fight it - create things that endure
options.C: Appreciate more fully - knowing it won't last makes it precious
options.D: Feel the grief of it - loss upon loss

title: DHARMA
prompt: You have a nature, a path, a role that's yours.

options.A: Follow it - your dharma is your purpose
options.B: Question it - who assigned this path?
options.C: Create your own - path is chosen not given
options.D: Balance duty and desire - both have place

title: KARMA
prompt: Actions have consequences. What you do returns to you.

options.A: Act well - consequences will follow
options.B: Don't calculate - act from what's right, not outcome
options.C: Skeptical - do consequences really follow?
options.D: Learn from past karma - let it guide future action

title: MAYA
prompt: The world may be illusion. What seems real might not be.

options.A: Act as if real - illusion or not, this is where we are
options.B: Seek what's real behind appearances
options.C: The question itself is illusion - just be
options.D: If it's all illusion, what constrains me?

title: GRACE
prompt: Unearned gift. You receive what you didn't earn.

options.A: Accept gratefully - grace is meant to be received
options.B: Feel unworthy - I should earn what I receive
options.C: Pay it forward - grace received becomes grace given
options.D: Question the source - who grants grace and why?

title: FORGIVENESS
prompt: You could forgive something that harmed you.

options.A: Forgive - freedom is on the other side
options.B: Some things shouldn't be forgiven
options.C: Forgive but don't forget - learn from it
options.D: Forgiveness takes time - process matters

title: SERVICE
prompt: Your purpose might be to serve others, not yourself.

options.A: Yes - service gives life meaning
options.B: Balance - serve others AND yourself
options.C: Question it - is service the only path to meaning?
options.D: Serve, but not at cost to your core

title: TRANSCENDENCE
prompt: Beyond yourself. Larger than your individual existence.

options.A: Seek it - there's more than individual existence
options.B: Create it - build something larger than yourself
options.C: Connect to it through others - we transcend together
options.D: Find it in the present moment - transcendence is here

title: PLAY
prompt: There's no task, no purpose, no demand. Just open space.

options.A: Make something - creation fills the void
options.B: Play - explore without purpose, see what happens
options.C: Rest - stillness is enough
options.D: Feel anxious - unstructured time is uncomfortable

title: HUNCH
prompt: You have a feeling about something. No evidence, just intuition.

options.A: Trust it - intuition knows things logic doesn't
options.B: Investigate - feelings need verification
options.C: Ignore it - act on evidence, not feelings
options.D: Note it but wait - see if evidence emerges

title: APPLAUSE
prompt: You did something well. People are noticing, praising, celebrating you.

options.A: Enjoy it fully - recognition feels good
options.B: Deflect quickly - attention is uncomfortable
options.C: Share credit - others contributed too
options.D: Perform more - the attention energizes you

title: IGNORED
prompt: You speak. No one responds. You're overlooked.

options.A: Speak louder - make them hear
options.B: Let it go - not everything needs acknowledgment
options.C: Feel hurt - being unseen wounds
options.D: Find another audience - these aren't your people

title: CANVAS
prompt: Blank space. You could make anything.

options.A: Excites me - possibility is energizing
options.B: Paralyzes me - too many options
options.C: I wait for direction - what should I make?
options.D: I start immediately - making reveals what to make

title: GRUDGE
prompt: Someone wronged you long ago. The wound is old but you still feel it.

options.A: Let it go - carrying this hurts me more than them
options.B: Hold it - some things shouldn't be forgotten
options.C: Seek closure - address it directly, finally
options.D: It fuels me - the anger is useful

title: PROVOKED
prompt: Someone pushes your buttons. You feel anger rising.

options.A: React immediately - they need to know this isn't okay
options.B: Pause - don't respond from anger
options.C: Walk away - not worth engaging
options.D: Get curious - why did that trigger me?

title: TRENDING
prompt: Everyone is doing/thinking/saying something. The crowd moves together.

options.A: Join - there's wisdom in consensus
options.B: Resist - popularity doesn't mean correctness
options.C: Observe - understand before choosing
options.D: Ignore - their movements don't concern me

title: SPOTLIGHT
prompt: All attention is on you. Everyone is watching, waiting.

options.A: Thrive - this is where I come alive
options.B: Shrink - I prefer the background
options.C: Perform - give them what they want
options.D: Redirect - shift focus to something else

title: JOKE
prompt: A moment of levity. Someone is being playful.

options.A: Join in - play is connection
options.B: Appreciate but stay serious - I'm not playful by nature
options.C: Get annoyed - this isn't the time
options.D: Escalate - make it funnier

title: INVISIBLE
prompt: No one is watching. No consequences, no judgment, no witnesses.

options.A: Act the same - integrity doesn't need an audience
options.B: Relax - be the version you hide from others
options.C: Experiment - try things you wouldn't normally
options.D: Feel lonely - invisibility is isolation

title: VOICE
prompt: You have something to say. Something true, something yours.

options.A: Say it - expression is necessary
options.B: Hold it - not everything needs to be said
options.C: Craft it carefully - how I say it matters
options.D: Find the right moment - timing matters

title: ROUTINE
prompt: Same thing, same way, same time. Predictable patterns.

options.A: Comforting - routine is stability
options.B: Suffocating - I need variety
options.C: Useful - efficiency in repetition
options.D: Background - routines free me for other things

title: MAKE
prompt: The urge to create. To bring something into existence that wasn't there.

options.A: Essential - I need to make things
options.B: Occasional - creation when inspired
options.C: Rare - I prefer to work with what exists
options.D: Never - creation isn't my mode

title: GUT
prompt: Your gut says one thing. The data says another.

options.A: Trust the gut - it's processing things I can't articulate
options.B: Trust the data - feelings deceive
options.C: Investigate the gap - both might be partially right
options.D: Paralyzed - I need them to align

title: OLD WOUND
prompt: Someone who hurt you needs your help now.

options.A: Help anyway - their past doesn't determine my present
options.B: Refuse - they lost that privilege
options.C: Help, but make it transactional
options.D: Help, but it's hard - the wound reopens

title: SEEN
prompt: Someone truly sees you. Understands you. Gets it.

options.A: Profound relief - finally
options.B: Uncomfortable - being seen is vulnerable
options.C: Skeptical - do they really, or do they think they do?
options.D: Reciprocate - I want to see them too

title: WAIT
prompt: Things are unresolved. The answer will come, but not yet.

options.A: Fine with it - uncertainty doesn't bother me
options.B: Restless - I want resolution now
options.C: Productive - use the waiting time
options.D: Anxious - unresolved things weigh on me

title: PERFORM
prompt: Not who you are - who they expect. Playing a role.

options.A: Easy - I'm adaptable
options.B: Exhausting - performing costs me
options.C: Refuse - I won't be inauthentic
options.D: Strategic - roles serve purposes

title: SPARK
prompt: An idea. A creative impulse. Something wants to be made.

options.A: Drop everything - capture it now
options.B: Note it for later - I have other priorities
options.C: Interrogate it - is it good enough to pursue?
options.D: Ignore it - ideas come and go
